items.title: Scoped access in Java
items.summary: I have published a tutorial about scoped access in Java. There is a video version of the tutorial too - linked from the textual version of the tutorial.

items.title: Method Chaining in Java
items.summary: I have published a tutorial about method chaining in Java. There is a video version of the tutorial too - linked from the textual version of the tutorial.

items.title: Java ConcurrentMap tutorial - updated
items.summary: I have udpated my Java ConcurrentMap tutorial - and added a video version of the tutorial.

items.title: What is new in Java 23
items.summary: I have updated my Java tutorial introduction to list what is new in Java 23. The list of new features now lists all the major features in each release, back to Java 7

items.title: Help Your Co-developers Grow - By Meeting Them Where They Are
items.summary: I have published a short video about helping your co-developers grow - by meeting them where they are and helping them grow from there. This advice may sound benign, but I have seen how well it works when done well, and I have also seen how poorly it works when developers ignore this advice, and scorn their co-developers for not having the "sufficient" know…

items.title: Java HttpClient - Updated
items.summary: I have updated my Java HttpClient tutorial to also show how to send an HTTP POST request to a web server.

items.title: P2P Networks Book Update
items.summary: I have updated my book about P2P Networks with a short chapter about the Kademlia P2P topology, and a few more details about Chord. More to come in the future.

items.title: Compositional Software Design
items.summary: I have finally "figured out" the core principles of what I call Compositional Software Design. I have published a tutorial explaining these core principles.There is still more to say about Compositional Software Design than what is currently in this tutorial - but there is enough to understand the central ideas. More will be added in the future, but will pr…

items.title: Daemon Threads in java
items.summary: I have just updated my tutorial about creating threads in Java, so it now also explains how to create Daemon threads.

items.title: Designing for Scalability vs. Performance
items.summary: I have made a short video about the difference between designing for scalability or performance. In other words, should you solve all capacity problems by throwing hardware at the problem? Or should you try to utilize the hardware you already have, better?

items.title: HTML5 Canvas : Images - Fixed
items.summary: I have fixed my HTML5 Canvas : Images tutorial - so the examples are actually drawn.

items.title: Java Generics - Updated
items.summary: I have updated my Java Generics introduction just now. Java Generics enables you to type safely create classes that can work on multiple different types.

items.title: HTML5 Canvas - Rectangles - Updated
items.summary: I have just fixed a small bug in the code examples in my HTML5 Canvas Rectangles tutorial - about how to draw rectangles on an HTML5 Canvas.

items.title: Python Classes
items.summary: I have just published a short tutorial on Python classes.

items.title: Java Generics Tutorial Video
items.summary: I have just published a video about Java Generics

items.title: Java 21 and 22 Features
items.summary: I have updated my Java introduction to list the major new features of Java 21 and 22. The page now lists all major features all the way back to Java 7.

items.title: Java Generic Methods - Updated
items.summary: I have clarified the explanations in my Java Generic Methods tutorial - so it is easier to understand what you can do.

items.title: Search Function on Jenkov.com
items.summary: I have added a search function to jenkov.com - after many requests to do so. It works well enough for you to be able to find most major topics on jenkov.com - and I keep improving it so you can find more and more. Try it out!

items.title: P2P Networks Introduction - Updated
items.summary: I have updated my P2P networks introduction to give a better overview of the core concepts and ideas.

items.title: Scalability vs. Performance?
items.summary: I have updated my Performance Optimization Principles tutorial to include a section discussing the difference between designing for scalability and performance - and which of the two approaches I prefer.

items.title: Java Ternary Operator Tutorial - updated
items.summary: The Java ternary operator lets you express conditional value assignment more concisely than by using if-statements. I have updated my Java ternary operator tutorial to also show how to use the ternary operator as a type check.

items.title: Python while Loop Tutorial
items.summary: I have published a Python while loop tutorial. While loops enable you to repeat a block of code as long as a certain condition is true.

items.title: Java ForkJoinPool Tutorial Updated
items.summary: I have updated my Java ForkJoinPool tutorial with simpler code examples, and a link to the Java ForkJoinPool video tutorial.

items.title: Java ForkJoinPool Video
items.summary: I've published a video version of my Java ForkJoinPool tutorial. The Java ForkJoinPool makes it easier to break down big tasks into smaller tasks which can be executed in parallel.

items.title: Python for Loop
items.summary: I've published a short tutorial on the Python for loop which is used to iterate sequences of elements such as lists, sets, tuples, dictionaries, ranges etc.

items.title: Python if
items.summary: I've published a short tutorial on the Python if instruction which is used to execute one or more instructions if a certain condition is met.

items.title: Python Data Types
items.summary: I've published a short tutorial on the core Python data types such as boolean, numeric, string, list, tuple, dictionary etc.

items.title: Python Variables
items.summary: I've published a short tutorial on the Python variables - about how to declare, read and set their values.

items.title: P2P Networks Introduction - Video
items.summary: I have published a P2P Networks Introduction video - covering some of the things that have happened since my first video about P2P networks 12 years ago. This includes different P2P topologies among other things - including my own Polymorph Polyring P2P topology.

items.title: Polymorph Data Language
items.summary: Polymorph Data Language is my own alternative to JSON, YAML, XML, CSV etc. It has most of their features + several new features combined into a single data language - having all the features I have alwways wanted a data language to have.

items.title: Java Concurrency Introduction - Updated
items.summary: Java gets new concurrency and multithreading constructs added regularly. Latest additions were Java Virtual Threads and Structured Concurrency. I will keep updating this tutorial accordingly.

items.title: Jackson ObjectMapper - Updated
items.summary: Smaller updates to my tutorial about the Jackson ObjectMapper - which can parse JSON into Java objects and vice versa.

items.title: Java Reflection - Finding Getters and Setters of a Class at Runtime
items.summary: It is possible to detect getters and setters of a Java class at runtime via Java Reflection. There are no direct methods for finding getters and setters built-in to the Java Reflection API, but you can still use it to detect getters and setters. I have updated this tutorial to be a bit more clear about how you do that!

items.title: Single Process Architecture
items.summary: I have updated my tutorial explaining single process architecture a bit - to be more clear about whether network communication makes an app not have a single process architecture or not.

items.title: Conscious Software Design - Video
items.summary: I have made a video explanation of my software design philsophy Conscious Software Design. In this video I go into more detail about what exactly this philosophy is all about. Hint: It's more about how to think about software design than about concrete techniques.

items.title: Java Microbenchmark Harness - Tutorial
items.summary: If you are doing performance optimization in Java, sometimes you will need to write some "microbenchmarks" that measure how fast a given implementation runs. Doing that so that the benchmarks actually measures the implementation fairly - is not easy! The Java Microbenchmark Harness can help you out here! This Java Microbenchmark Harness tutorial explains ho…

items.title: Conscious Software Design - Consciousness Levels
items.summary: I have updated my tutorial about conscious software design to include a list of 4 different conscioussness levels I believe developers and architects tend to operate on: Oblivious, unconscious, subconscious and conscious.

items.title: The SOLID Principles
items.summary: Here are my notes about the SOLID design principles so far. I will probably be adding some comments to them over time, as I learn more about software design - but here is the first version.

items.title: Structural Software Design
items.summary: I have just published a short tutorial where I lay out the basics of structural software design. There is a video version of the tutorial too - linked from inside the tutorial.

items.title: Software Design Goals
items.summary: New article about software design goals - the commonly occurring reasons behind why we make the design decisions we do. There is both a textual and a video version of the article. There is a link to the video from within the article.

items.title: Software Design Introduction
items.summary: I have finished my software design introduction - laying out what I believe to be the fundamental concepts of software design. More tutorials with more details will come in the future. There is a textual and video version.

items.title: Java volatile Tutorial - Corrected
items.summary: I have corrected my Java volatile tutorial - so that it no longer talks about the "CPU cache" - but "CPU registers" instead. Both text and diagrams have been fixed ?

items.title: Undertow Java Web Server Introduction
items.summary: I have published a short introduction to the Undertow Java web server. Undertow is the web server used internally in JBoss Wildfly. Undertow is a minmalistic web server which can be embedded into your own Java applications. You can package your project as a single fat jar, from as little as 5-6 MB . Undertow has great performance, offers both a non-blocking…

items.title: The Compound Performance Penalty Problem - Video Edition
items.summary: The Compound Performance Penalty Problem - about how using more and more third party software in your apps will often come with a performance penalty. With many third party libs - that penalty compounds.

items.title: ExecutorService Using Java Virtual Threads Internally - Video Edition
items.summary: I have created a video showing how to create a Java ExecutorService that uses virtual threads internally.

items.title: ExecutorService Using Java Virtual Threads Internally
items.summary: I have updated my Java ExecutorService tutorial to show how to create an ExecutorService that uses virtual threads internally.

items.title: Java Virtual Threads - Video Edition
items.summary: I have published a video version of my Java Virtual Threads tutorial.

items.title: Java Virtual Threads
items.summary: I have published my first notes on Java virtual threads. Enough to get you started playing with them. More will come later!

items.title: Programming Paradigms
items.summary: I have collected a list of the most common programming paradigms I have encountered in my 20+ years of professional software development. This list contains paradigms such as structured programming, functional programming, object oriented programming, data oriented programming etc.

items.title: How to Choose What Programming Languages to Learn?
items.summary: With all the programming languages available out there today, it can be hard to choose which programming languages to learn! Therefore I have published my own criteria for choosing what programming languages I choose to learn myself.

items.title: Java Thread Signaling - Updated With Video
items.summary: I have updated my Java Thread signaling tutorial, among other things with a video version of the tutorial.

items.title: Evolutionary Software Development
items.summary: Evolutionary software development is the term I am using for the development process I see many startups and also larger corporations actually use, even though they call it Scrum or Kanban on the surface. This article explains the three core principles of evolutionary software development.

items.title: UFT-8 Tutorial Updated
items.summary: I have updated my UTF-8 tutorial to also show how to read and write UTF-8 in Java.

items.title: Unicode Tutorial Cleaned Up
items.summary: I have cleaned up my Unicode tutorial so it is now easier to get an overview of Unicode.

items.title: AWS CloudFormation Parameters
items.summary: AWS CloudFormation templates can have parameters which can be filled in at the time the CloudFormation template is deployed, making it possible to use the same AWS CloudFormation template to create different (but similar) stacks.

items.title: AWS CloudFormation Introduction
items.summary: I have started writing about AWS CloudFormation which is one of the AWS IaC (Infrastructure as Code) tools made available for free by AWS.

items.title: Java Generics - Updated
items.summary: I have started updating my Java Generics tutorial

items.title: AWS Core Technologies
items.summary: With hundreds of AWS services and technologies available, it can be hard to figure out which technologies to learn first, and which can wait. Therefore I have published a list of what I consider core AWS technologies which I believe it is good to know early in your AWS journey. These technologies are often used, so learning them will be beneficial.

items.title: AWS Introduction
items.summary: If you are just beginning your AWS journey - figuring out which AWS services to learn first, and how to interact best with AWS can be a daunting task. Therefore I have published a short introduction to AWS which lists the bare essentials to guide you on this AWS journey.

items.title: Developer Learning Strategies
items.summary: As developer we have to learn a lot continuously. Learning can sometimes be frustrating. I have published an article explaining my developer learning strategies - for dealing with the times when learning is hard.
